categoryDescription:
For the first time ever ‘Sports’ was introduced in the Club’s calendar of events in the year 1986. Our country’s most popular sport ‘Cricket’ made an entry into the Ad Club Madras with the support of Sportstar from The Hindu Group of Publications. Each year the popularity of this event grew and is today a much awaited event, attracting 25 to 30 teams, with the winners walking with the Prestigious ‘Ad Club Sportstar’ Trophy.

Cricket was soon followed by Table Tennis and Ball Badminton. Table Tennis was reintroduced in 1997-98 with 18 teams participating at the University Union Indoor stadium. The Winners’ Trophy was annexed by O&M. The event continues, attracting participants from both corporate and individual members of the club. The year 1999-2000 saw the addition of three more events like Chess, Carroms and Bowling.

The story goes that a Dublin theatre proprietor by the name Richard Daly made a bet that he could, within forty-eight hours, make a nonsense word known throughout the city, and that the public would give a meaning to it. After the performance one evening, he gave off his staff cards with the word ‘Quiz’ written on them and told them to write the word on walls around the city. The next day the strange word was the talk of the town and within a short time had become part of the language.

Everyone these days is interested in quizzing. Quizzing is celebrated by popular television programs like Kaun Banega Crorepathi, Kya Aap paanchvi paas se tez hain, Dus Ka dum etc dishing out huge sums in prize money. While the not so prize-rich quiz contests like Landmark, Odyssey, IIT open, Tata Crucible etc., are made popular by the purists and enthusiasts.

The Ad Club Businessline Quiz programme sponsored by The Hindu Businessline is hugely popular among the advertising fraternity with executives offering to participate from far away cities like Cochin, Bangalore, Hyderabad and Rourkela.

Creative excellence deserves to be honoured. The Ad Club Madras began this tradition as early as 1977, and the recently rechristened Ad Club Madras Maddys Awards have recognized and motivated a multitude of creative talent.

The awards span across more than 40 categories and three awards – Gold, Silver and Bronze – are given in each category. In recent years the Best Copywriter of the year, Best Art Director of the year and the Agency of the year were also recognized and felicitated.

As The Hindu reports read “the creative output is put to the final test when the best bring home the bacon and the deafening applause and shrieks of the victorious, momentarily breaking away from the shackles of demanding clients, pierce the still of the night.”

Advertising Club Madras is relaunching its Tea Meetings in a brand new avatar called “AdTalks”. AdTalks will feature talks from leaders of the Advertising, Media and Marketing industry to share their experiences and inspirations.
